name: TrustMap Layer 1 Graphical Asset Redesign
public_explainer: The TrustMap shows the decision at the center of the digital trust universe. Detail increases as you move outward. Select any trust asset to see why it affects the decision.
ui_language_rule: Do not show registry, build version, render source, or implementation language in executive-facing TrustMap UI. Keep that language in Settings and repo documentation only.
score_rule: Every visible score must be clickable and must open or route to the model, evidence, assumptions, missing inputs, confidence, reliance risk, and decision rationale behind that score.

active_decision.label: Active Decision
active_decision.statement: A business action is under review and requires trust verification before reliance.
active_decision.summary: CyberShield is evaluating whether this action can be trusted before consequence.

layer1_assets.label: Cloud & Infrastructure
layer1_assets.relationship: Infrastructure hosting, network path, and cloud controls affect decision trust.
layer1_assets.next_action: Confirm hosting, network, and cloud-control evidence.

layer1_assets.label: Identities & Access
layer1_assets.relationship: Requester authority, access rights, and approval path determine action admissibility.
layer1_assets.next_action: Verify who is requesting the action and whether they are authorized.

layer1_assets.label: Applications & Data
layer1_assets.relationship: Data sensitivity, application context, and permitted use determine whether the action can proceed.
layer1_assets.next_action: Confirm data class, purpose, and application owner approval.

layer1_assets.label: AI Systems & Agents
layer1_assets.relationship: AI output, model provenance, input context, tool access, and human review determine reliance.
layer1_assets.next_action: Verify AI-generated analysis before business action.

layer1_assets.label: Third Parties & Vendors
layer1_assets.relationship: Vendor proof, contractual obligations, external use, and third-party controls can break trust.
layer1_assets.next_action: Verify vendor evidence and terms before reliance.

layer1_assets.label: Devices & Endpoints
layer1_assets.relationship: Endpoint health and device posture affect whether the action or data path can be trusted.
layer1_assets.next_action: Confirm endpoint health and control evidence.

layer1_assets.label: CMMC & Compliance
layer1_assets.relationship: Control obligations, audit defensibility, contractual exposure, and compliance evidence determine whether the decision can be defended.
layer1_assets.next_action: Confirm control impact and compliance evidence before reliance.

dead_drilldown_repairs.trigger_text: employee or business unit wants to act on AI generated analysis
dead_drilldown_repairs.summary: A business unit wants to act on AI-generated analysis. CyberShield must verify the model, evidence, authority, reliance risk, and human review path before action.
dead_drilldown_repairs.next_action: Open Runtime to decide whether the action is allowed, constrained, escalated, or refused.

score_explanation_targets.meaning: Current decision posture score based on trust, confidence, evidence, reliance risk, and runtime admissibility.
Distribution of Layer 1 trust assets by state. This is not the same as the Operational Trust Score.

trust_distribution_rule: Trust Level Distribution must show counts or percentages across Layer 1 assets, not duplicate the Operational Trust Score number.
layering_rule: The radar and constellation background is the environment. The Layer 1 graphical trust assets are the interface. The active decision is the gravity center.
boundary: Static advisory visual and interaction layer only. No live scoring, live evidence retrieval, workflow automation, ticketing, notification, backend persistence, enforcement, statistical validation, certification, compliance validation, or live integrations.
